sections.heading: Endometrial Carcinoma with Disease Limited to the Uterus (Pure Endometrioid Histology)
sections.content:
Branching basis: Histologic grade, Depth of myometrial invasion, LVSI, Age, Molecular profile

Node 1 criteria: Medically operable patients with clinically uterine-confined disease.
Options:
- Surgery: TH/BSO with surgical staging (Preferred). Minimally invasive surgery is preferred when feasible. Includes peritoneal lavage, lymph node assessment (SLN mapping preferred), and omental biopsy for high-risk histologies.
- Radiation: Adjuvant RT based on stage and risk factors (Risk-adapted). For Stage IA G1-2: observation or vaginal brachytherapy if LVSI or age ≥60y. For Stage IA G3: vaginal brachytherapy preferred. For Stage IB: vaginal brachytherapy preferred, with consideration of EBRT for higher risk factors. For Stage II: EBRT preferred with or without brachytherapy.
Adjuvant logic: Adjuvant therapy is based on surgical-pathologic findings: Stage I (observation or vaginal brachytherapy), Stage II (EBRT ± brachytherapy ± systemic therapy), Stages III-IV (systemic therapy ± EBRT ± vaginal brachytherapy). Molecular testing may influence decisions, e.g., POLE-mutated tumors may de-escalate therapy.

Node 2 criteria: Medically inoperable patients.
Options:
- Radiation: Definitive RT with or without brachytherapy (Preferred). EBRT to pelvis with or without para-aortic RT, combined with brachytherapy. For uterine-limited disease, brachytherapy alone may be considered for selected cases.
- Systemic_therapy: Hormonal therapy for select patients (Useful in certain circumstances). Progestin-based therapy (e.g., levonorgestrel IUD, megestrol, medroxyprogesterone) for low-grade endometrioid histology with ER/PR positivity.
Adjuvant logic: N/A - primary definitive treatment.

sections.heading: Endometrial Carcinoma with Suspected or Gross Cervical Involvement
sections.content:
Branching basis: Cervical biopsy results, Resectability, Performance status

Node 1 criteria: Negative cervical biopsy or MRI.
Options:
- Surgery: TH or radical hysterectomy with BSO and surgical staging (Preferred). Radical hysterectomy may be performed if needed for negative margins.
Adjuvant logic: Treat as disease limited to uterus with adjuvant therapy based on final staging (ENDO-4, ENDO-5, ENDO-6).

Node 2 criteria: Positive cervical stromal involvement.
Options:
- Surgery: TH/BSO with surgical staging (Preferred for operable patients). Alternative: EBRT ± brachytherapy followed by TH/BSO 4–12 weeks post-RT.
- Radiation: EBRT ± brachytherapy ± systemic therapy (For inoperable patients). Definitive RT with re-evaluation for surgery if rendered operable.
Adjuvant logic: For surgically staged Stage II: EBRT preferred with or without brachytherapy and ± systemic therapy (category 2B).

sections.heading: Endometrial Carcinoma with Suspected Extrauterine Disease
sections.content:
Branching basis: Imaging findings, Resectability, Histology

Node 1 criteria: No evidence of extrauterine disease on imaging.
Options:
- Surgery: TH/BSO with surgical staging (Preferred). Treat as disease limited to uterus.
Adjuvant logic: Based on surgical staging.

Node 2 criteria: Abdominal/pelvic-confined disease.
Options:
- Surgery: TH/BSO with surgical staging/debulking (Preferred). Goal: no measurable residual disease. Preoperative systemic therapy can be considered.
Adjuvant logic: Adjuvant therapy for Stage III–IV: systemic therapy ± EBRT ± vaginal brachytherapy.

Node 3 criteria: Distant metastases.
Options:
- Systemic_therapy: Systemic therapy ± EBRT ± SBRT ± TH/BSO (Preferred). Consider ablation for 1–5 metastatic lesions if primary cancer controlled.
Adjuvant logic: Palliative intent.

flowcharts.title: Endometrial Carcinoma with Disease Limited to the Uterus (Pure Endometrioid Histology)

nodes.label: Medically operable patients with clinically uterine-confined disease. [Adjuvant summary: Adjuvant therapy is based on surgical-pathologic findings: Stage I (observation or vaginal brachytherapy), Stage II (EBRT ± brachytherapy ± systemic therapy), Stages III-IV (systemic therapy ± EBRT ± vaginal brachytherapy). Molecular testing may influence decisions, e.g., POLE-mutated tumors may de-escalate therapy.]

TH/BSO with surgical staging | Preferred | Minimally invasive surgery is preferred when feasible. Includes peritoneal lavage, lymph node assessment (SLN mapping preferred), and omental biopsy for high-risk histologies.

Adjuvant RT based on stage and risk factors | Risk-adapted | For Stage IA G1-2: observation or vaginal brachytherapy if LVSI or age ≥60y. For Stage IA G3: vaginal brachytherapy preferred. For Stage IB: vaginal brachytherapy preferred, with consideration of EBRT for higher risk factors. For Stage II: EBRT preferred with or without brachytherapy.
Medically inoperable patients. [Adjuvant summary: N/A - primary definitive treatment.]

Definitive RT with or without brachytherapy | Preferred | EBRT to pelvis with or without para-aortic RT, combined with brachytherapy. For uterine-limited disease, brachytherapy alone may be considered for selected cases.

Hormonal therapy for select patients | Useful in certain circumstances | Progestin-based therapy (e.g., levonorgestrel IUD, megestrol, medroxyprogesterone) for low-grade endometrioid histology with ER/PR positivity.
